nodes.title: Render one step from the current index
nodes.why: Built-in @switch keeps the form in one component while exposing only the active step content.
nodes.alternative: Mount every step at once and hide inactive sections with CSS.
nodes.tradeoff: Step DOM is recreated during navigation, but the template state is explicit and easier to test.

nodes.title: Validate the current group before advancing
nodes.why: Only the active step is surfaced and validated before currentStep can move forward.
nodes.alternative: Advance first and reveal validation errors on the next screen.
nodes.tradeoff: Users must resolve current-step errors before continuing, which protects final form completeness.

nodes.title: Expose the same next-step contract in the UI
nodes.why: The button state reflects the derived validity rule while the method guard remains the final protection.
nodes.alternative: Enable Next unconditionally and depend only on click-time validation.
nodes.tradeoff: Disabled controls communicate readiness early, though inline errors are still needed to explain why.

nodes.title: Validate the full form at submission
nodes.why: Final submission rechecks every nested group and exposes any invalid field before reading the form value.
nodes.alternative: Assume step navigation guarantees validity forever.
nodes.tradeoff: The final validation is redundant in the happy path, but protects against programmatic state changes.
